title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
legalCaveat: Security references support control mapping and evidence requests. They are not a certification decision.
summary: Reference manifest for mapping supplier and platform controls to cybersecurity governance, risk, asset, identity, incident, and recovery questions.
excerpt: Use as advisory reference context for security control discovery, SOC2-style evidence requests, access control checks, incident response, and recovery obligations.

title: NIST SP 800-53 Security and Privacy Controls
legalCaveat: Security references support control mapping and evidence requests. They are not a certification decision.
summary: Reference manifest for common security and privacy control families used during evidence and assurance review.
excerpt: Use as advisory context for asking about access control, audit logging, configuration management, incident response, risk assessment, and system protection controls.

title: NIST SP 800-171 Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information
legalCaveat: Security references support control mapping and evidence requests. They are not a certification decision.
summary: Reference manifest for supplier handling of controlled or sensitive information.
excerpt: Use as advisory context when vendor or outsourcing cases involve sensitive information, subcontractors, identity access, encryption, incident reporting, or controlled records.

title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
legalCaveat: AI governance references support model-risk and Responsible AI review. They do not grant production authorization.
summary: Reference manifest for Responsible AI and model-risk questions.
excerpt: Use as advisory context for AI workflow cases involving model training, automated decisioning, human oversight, transparency, bias testing, monitoring, and accountability.

title: NIST Privacy Framework
legalCaveat: Compliance references are public guidance context only. Applicability must be confirmed by accountable legal, privacy, and compliance owners.
summary: Reference manifest for privacy-risk management questions.
excerpt: Use as advisory context for privacy governance, personal-data inventory, data processing basis, retention, deletion, cross-border processing, and privacy-control evidence.
